mission.title: Install and Configure ClickHouse
mission.description: ClickHouse is a real-time analytics database management system designed for generating analytical data reports. It is ideal for scenarios requiring high performance and scalability for large datasets. Key trade-offs include the need for careful schema design and potential complexity in managing distributed setups.

steps.title: Add ClickHouse Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official ClickHouse Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install ClickHouse using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add clickhouse https://clickhouse.github.io/clickhouse-operator

helm repo update

```

> This adds the ClickHouse operator repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the ClickHouse operator in the specified namespace, which manages ClickHouse clusters.

```bash

kubectl create namespace clickhouse

helm install clickhouse-operator clickhouse/clickhouse-operator --namespace clickhouse --version 0.24.0

```

> Creates a namespace for ClickHouse.
> Installs the ClickHouse operator with a specific version.

steps.title: Deploy ClickHouse Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a ClickHouse cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: clickhouse-cluster
  namespace: clickhouse
spec:
  configuration:
    clusters:
      - name: cluster
        layout:
          shardsCount: 1
          replicasCount: 1
EOF

```

> Deploys a simple ClickHouse cluster configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: clickhouse-cluster
  namespace: clickhouse
spec:
  configuration:
    clusters:
      - name: cluster
        layout:
          shardsCount: 1
          replicasCount: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify ClickHouse Cluster
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the ClickHouse cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clickhouseclusters -n clickhouse

kubectl describe clickhousecluster clickhouse-cluster -n clickhouse

```

> Lists ClickHouse clusters in the specified namespace.
> Describes the ClickHouse cluster for detailed status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ClickHouse Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ClickHouse operator and the associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all ClickHouse clusters and their data.

```bash

helm uninstall clickhouse-operator --namespace clickhouse

kubectl delete namespace clickhouse

```

> Uninstalls the ClickHouse operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ClickHouse Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade the ClickHouse operator to a new version.

```bash

kubectl get clickhouseclusters -n clickhouse -o yaml > backup-clickhouse-clusters.yaml

helm upgrade clickhouse-operator clickhouse/clickhouse-operator --namespace clickhouse --version 0.25.0

kubectl get clickhouseclusters -n clickhouse

```

> Backs up current ClickHouse cluster configurations.
> Upgrades the ClickHouse operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the ClickHouse clusters are still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get clickhouseclusters -n clickhouse -o yaml > backup-clickhouse-clusters.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create ClickHouse cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check resource limits and ensure the cluster configuration is correct.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused to ClickHouse server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse server may not be running or is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Verify the status of the ClickHouse pods using 'kubectl get pods -n clickhouse'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the ClickHouse operator has not been installed or has been uninstalled.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the ClickHouse operator using the Helm install command.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse pods may not have enough memory allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the memory limits in the ClickHouse cluster configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new data types and improved query performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old configuration format for clusters.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v25.x, update your cluster configurations to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the ClickHouse cluster running with the command 'kubectl get clickhouseclusters -n clickhouse' returning the status as 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: clickhouse-cluster
  namespace: clickhouse
spec:
  configuration:
    clusters:
      - name: cluster
        layout:
          shardsCount: 1
          replicasCount: 1
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster up and running with sufficient resources.
